items.title: Thought this, from @thesteinline.bsky.social's latest, was an interesting framing of the Lakers' Walker Kessler deal: marcstein.substack.com/p/another-bu...
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt:
Thought this, from @thesteinline.bsky.social's latest, was an interesting framing of the Lakers' Walker Kessler deal: marcstein.substack.com/p/another-bu...

I can remember hearing (maybe making?) similar arguments about the draft-pick price for Bridges being evened out by not giving up any for OG.

images.alt:
To establish a pathway so quickly after the marketplace opened to pry Kessler away from the Jazz required them to surrender two future first-round picks (fully unprotected in both 2031 and 2033) and two first-round pick swaps (2028 and 2030) in addition to signing Kessler to a new four-year, $130 million contract.

Yet I'm told that the Lakers were emboldened to go to such lengths to acquire Kessler in large part because they acquired Luka Dončić from Dallas on such favorable terms in February 2025.

As hard to believe as it continues to be nearly 18 months since The Trade was consummated, Dončić was somehow pilfered from the Mavericks with just one unprotected first-round pick in 2029 leaving Lakerland. That solitary future first was packaged with Anthony Davis and Max Christie to land Luka ... with the Lakers also successfully keeping Austin Reaves out of the trade.

The trade assets preserved in that heist, in other words, helped nudge the Lakers to a place where they believed they could withstand spending so big on Kessler.

items.title: July 20, 1986: Arvydas Sabonis with a putback dunk over David Robinson during the gold medal game at the World Basketball Championship.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
July 20, 1986: Arvydas Sabonis with a putback dunk over David Robinson during the gold medal game at the World Basketball Championship.

Sabonis—selected by Portland in the first round of the NBA Draft a month earlier—had 16 PTS/11 REB for the USSR.

The United States won, 87-85.

Walsh will be extension-eligible throughout the balance of the season. He can extend for up to 140% of the average player salary, which would start at $21.2M per season.
